report_name: Critical Cisco IMC Flaw (CVE-2026-20093) Allows Full Server Takeover

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-20093
vulnerabilities.description: A critical authentication bypass vulnerability in Cisco's Integrated Management Controller (IMC) firmware. The flaw exists in the XML API and allows a remote, unauthenticated attacker to reset the password of any user account, including administrative ones, by sending a single crafted HTTP POST request to the 'configConfMo' method.
affected_products: Cisco UCS C-Series M5 Rack Servers
Cisco UCS C-Series M6 Rack Servers
Cisco 5000 Series Enterprise Network Compute Systems (ENCS)
Cisco Catalyst 8300 Series Edge uCPE platforms

indicators.name: Cisco IMC Auth Bypass Exploit Attempt (CVE-2026-20093)
indicators.pattern:
title: Cisco IMC Auth Bypass Exploit Attempt (CVE-2026-20093)
id: 8b1c9d5e-4f3a-4e8b-9c1d-0a2b3c4d5e6f
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects potential exploitation attempts against the Cisco IMC authentication bypass vulnerability (CVE-2026-20093).
  This rule looks for POST requests to the vulnerable XML API endpoint.
  Detection of the specific payload ('configConfMo', 'aaaUser') requires deep packet inspection or WAF capabilities that can inspect the POST body.
references:
  - https://www.cycognito.com/blog/emerging-threat-cve-2026-20093-cisco-imc-authentication-bypass
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-06
tags:
  - cve.2026.20093
  - attack.t1190
level: high
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    cs-method: 'POST'
    c-uri|endswith: '/imc/xml-api'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects HTTP POST requests to the vulnerable /imc/xml-api endpoint, which could indicate an attempt to exploit the Cisco IMC authentication bypass vulnerability CVE-2026-20093.

relationships.source_name: Cisco IMC Auth Bypass Exploit Attempt (CVE-2026-20093)
